hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and hydrophobic packing at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 residues while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid, but we must refine the focus on selectivity by targeting non-pharmacophore positions for mutations to reduce off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: Tightening the ddG threshold will filter out weaker binders and focus on candidates with stronger SSTR2 binding energy.
To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt off-target interactions at conserved positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are not part of the pharmacophore while preserving FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
